title: P1 fix: align write-guard's flag-value parsing with the dispatcher (--prefixed values)
action: P1 fix (review-phase-1.md, corroborated by code-quality + security reviewers): parseCliFlags's nextIsValue check currently refuses to consume a next token as a flag's value when that token starts with '--', even for a non-boolean-schema flag - diverging from bee.mjs's parseFlags, which always consumes the next token as the value for any non-FLAG_ALONE_BOOLEANS flag. Fix: for a non-boolean-schema flag, consume the next token as the value whenever it exists, regardless of a leading '--' (only fall back to treating the flag as boolean when next is undefined). Add a regression test asserting a call like 'bee_decisions.mjs log --decision "--foo" --rationale bar' is NOT denied by the hook.

truths: A call with a flag value that legitimately begins with '--' (e.g. --decision "--foo") is validated the same way by the hook and by bee.mjs's own validate-args.mjs (both accept it)
Existing gate/reservation/privacy guard tests and the existing CLI-shape tests still pass unchanged
key_links: new regression test in test_bee_write_guard_hook.mjs exercises the fixed path
prohibitions: No change to the gate-guard, reservation-guard, or privacy/scout-guard logic - isolated to parseCliFlags only

trace.outcome: Fixed write-guard's parseCliFlags to consume the next token as a value regardless of leading --, matching bee.mjs's parseFlags exactly

  "tests_inspected": ["skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs", "skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_write_guard_hook.mjs"],
  "tests_added_or_changed": ["skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_write_guard_hook.mjs (added 1 regression test: a flag value beginning with -- is consumed correctly, not misread as a new flag)"],
  "red_failure_evidence": "Ran the pre-fix hook (git show HEAD:hooks/bee-write-guard.mjs, before this cell's edit) against this repo's real .bee/ environment with the exact call 'node .bee/bin/bee_decisions.mjs log --decision \"--foo\" --rationale bar': exit code 2, stderr 'bee CLI-shape guard: ... does not match decisions.log's schema - invalid type, expected string (field: decision)' - the hook misread --decision's value as a boolean flag instead of consuming \"--foo\" as its string value. After the fix, the identical call exits 0 (allowed), matching bee.mjs's own dispatcher which always accepted this call.",
  "verification_run": "node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_lib.mjs -> 124 passed, 0 failed. node skills/bee-hive/templates/tests/test_bee_write_guard_hook.mjs -> 16 passed, 0 failed (15 pre-existing + 1 new regression test for this fix).",
  "deliberate_exceptions": []
}
